codeReview.summary: Feature fully implemented. post_session_learning.rs (786 lines) provides LearnedPattern, ToolCallRecord, TaskTrajectory, PostSessionLearner, and PostSessionLearningService. Trajectory analysis extracts tool sequences using sliding windows (2-5 tools), counts occurrences, and stores patterns with confidence scores. 17 comprehensive tests cover extraction, confidence scoring, validation boost, and persistence. All 425 swell-memory tests pass. Clippy clean.

issues.description: LearnedPattern::new() sets confidence to 0.0 initially, then build_patterns calls update_confidence(). This is correct but could be simplified - new() could initialize with update_confidence() called immediately.

generate_pattern_name truncates sequences > 50 chars by taking first 47 chars + '...pattern', resulting in names like 'read_file_edit_file...pattern'. The label check in get_patterns looks for ends_with('...pattern') which works, but a more robust approach might use a consistent prefix like 'post_session_'.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Feature specifies skillName 'rust-worker' but the skill file was not found at .factory/skills/rust-worker/SKILL.md. Transcript skeleton references mission-worker-base and rust-worker skills, but only .factory/skills/rust/SKILL.md exists in the project skills directory. Cannot verify if worker followed the correct procedure.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: jq --arg id 'mem-post-session-learning' returned skillName='rust-worker' but ls .factory/skills/rust-worker/ returned ENOENT. Glob found skills at .factory/skills/rust/ but not rust-worker.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Feature fulfills VAL-MEM-006 but this assertion was not visible in the validation contract excerpt provided. The scrutiny validator should verify VAL-MEM-006 is documented in the mission's validation contract.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json shows 'fulfills': ['VAL-MEM-006']' but grep for VAL-MEM-006 in validation-contract.md returned no matches in the visible excerpt.

summary: mem-post-session-learning passes scrutiny review. Implementation is complete: PostSessionLearner analyzes task trajectories, extracts tool sequences via sliding windows (length 2-5), counts occurrences, and stores patterns with confidence scores (formula: min(observations/10, 1.0) + 0.1 if validated, capped at 0.95). Patterns are stored as MemoryEntry blocks with Skill block type and label prefix 'pattern:'. Retrieval via get_patterns filters by repository and parses only entries with labels ending in '...pattern'. 17 tests validate all core functionality. Two minor non-blocking observations: (1) confidence initialization could be consolidated in new(), (2) pattern label convention relies on '...pattern' suffix which is fragile but functional. Worker skill file not found - could not verify procedure adherence. Feature fulfills VAL-MEM-006 but assertion not found in visible contract excerpt.
